So the code can be written as:

int minSteps(int n) {

    if(n ==1) return 0;

    int sum=0;

    for(int i=2; i*i <=n; i++){

        while(n%i ==0){

            sum +=i;

            n /=i;

        }

    }

    if(n>1) sum +=n;

    return sum;

}

So, the code is as follows:

class Solution {
public:
    int minSteps(int n) {
        if (n == 1) return 0;
        int sum = 0;
        for (int i = 2; i * i <= n; ++i) {
            while (n % i == 0) {
                sum += i;
                n /= i;
            }
        }
        if (n > 1) sum += n;
        return sum;
    }
};

This should handle all cases correctly, including n=5, which returns 5 steps.

solution: 
```class Solution {
public:
    int minSteps(int n) {
        if (n == 1) return 0;
        int sum = 0;
        for (int i = 2; i * i <= n; ++i) {
            while (n % i == 0) {
                sum += i;
                n /= i;
            }
        }
        if (n > 1) sum += n;
        return sum;
    }
};
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(sqrt(n))
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution efficiently computes the minimum steps by summing the prime factors of n. This approach ensures that each copy operation is followed by the maximum possible pastes, minimizing the total steps required. The algorithm runs in O(sqrt(n)) time due to prime factorization, making it highly efficient for the given problem constraints.
